ucs.title: [LLM] Ajax Security Team / Rocket Kitten — Saffron Rose macro-doc to LOLBin ingress chain
ucs.description: Hunts the signature spearphishing-attachment delivery chain used by Ajax Security Team (Operation Saffron Rose / Operation Woolen-Goldfish): an Office application spawning a script interpreter or LOLBin that immediately reaches out to fetch the second-stage 'Stealer' implant — frequently themed around fake anti-censorship / VPN tooling (Psiphon, Proxifier, Anti-Censor, etc.) used to lure Iranian dissidents and US DIB targets.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as path values(Processes.process_hash) as hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","mspub.exe","wordpad.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where match(cmdline,"(?i)(https?://|ftp://|downloadstring|downloadfile|invoke-webrequest|iex |\\-enc |bitsadmin\\s+.*?/transfer|certutil\\s+.*?-urlcache|webclient|psiphon|proxifier|anti.?censor|saffron|stealer|woolen|goldfish)")
| where NOT match(user,"(?i)\\$$")
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| table firstTime lastTime host user parent_process_name process_name cmdline path hash count
ucs.kql:
// Ajax Security Team — Saffron Rose / Woolen-Goldfish macro-attachment chain
// Office app -> LOLBin/script interpreter -> remote fetch of stage-2 Stealer
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","mspub.exe","wordpad.exe"]);
let LolBinChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
| where FileName in~ (LolBinChildren)
// Network-fetch primitives OR Ajax/Rocket-Kitten campaign vocabulary (anti-censorship lures)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","ftp://","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX","WebClient","bitsadmin","certutil")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(psiphon|proxifier|anti.?censor|saffron|stealer|woolen|goldfish|vpn[- _]install)"
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-(e|en|enc|encodedcommand)\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Ajax Security Team — Saffron Rose / Woolen-Goldfish macro-attachment chain
// Sentinel SecurityEvent (EventID 4688 process-create) port. Requires "Include command line in process creation events" GPO.
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","mspub.exe","wordpad.exe"]);
let LolBinChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (OfficeParents)
| where ChildLeaf in (LolBinChildren)
| where CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","ftp://","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX","WebClient","bitsadmin","certutil")
   or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(psiphon|proxifier|anti.?censor|saffron|stealer|woolen|goldfish|vpn[- _]install)"
   or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-(e|en|enc|encodedcommand)\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Office Application Spawns LOLBin With Remote-Fetch Indicator (Ajax Security Team / Rocket Kitten)
id: 8e2c4a3a-3a55-4c7c-9f10-ajax01saffron
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects Office applications spawning script interpreters or LOLBins whose command line contains a remote-download primitive,
  optionally combined with Ajax Security Team campaign vocabulary (Psiphon/Proxifier/anti-censor/Saffron/Stealer/Woolen/Goldfish).
  Maps to the documented Operation Saffron Rose and Operation Woolen-Goldfish delivery chains.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0130
  - https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2014/05/operation-saffron-rose.html
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/vinfo/us/security/news/cyber-attacks/operation-woolen-goldfish-when-kittens-go-phishing
author: Detection Engineering (Ajax/Rocket-Kitten profile)
date: 2026/05/05
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\mspub.exe'
      - '\wordpad.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
  selection_net_primitive:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - 'ftp://'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'DownloadFile'
      - 'Invoke-WebRequest'
      - 'IEX'
      - 'WebClient'
      - 'bitsadmin'
      - 'certutil -urlcache'
      - '-EncodedCommand'
  selection_lure_terms:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'psiphon'
      - 'proxifier'
      - 'anti-censor'
      - 'saffron'
      - 'stealer'
      - 'woolen'
      - 'goldfish'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and (selection_net_primitive or selection_lure_terms)
fields:
  - User
  - ParentImage
  - Image
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned macro automation pulling content from internal SharePoint / intranet over https
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.g0130
ucs.rationale: Ajax Security Team's documented intrusions (Operation Saffron Rose, Operation Woolen-Goldfish) consistently relied on Office macro attachments themed as fake anti-censorship/VPN installers (Psiphon, Proxifier, Anti-Censor) targeting Iranian dissidents and US DIB targets, dropping the 'Stealer' family as second-stage. The parent=Office + child=LOLBin + remote-fetch combination — especially when pai

_field_issues.message: `Za` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `Za` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Ajax 'Stealer' browser-credential database exfiltration by non-browser process
ucs.description: Hunts the signature post-exploitation behaviour of Ajax Security Team's 'Stealer' family: a non-browser process copying Chrome/Edge/Firefox credential SQLite databases out of the user's profile directory for exfiltration. Catches T1555.003 the way Stealer actually performs it (file-copy of Login Data / logins.json) rather than at the API level.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as path values(Filesystem.process_name) as process from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created AND (Filesystem.file_name IN ("Login Data","Web Data","Cookies","logins.json","key3.db","key4.db","signons.sqlite","cert8.db","cert9.db","places.sqlite")) AND NOT Filesystem.process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","setup.exe","msiexec.exe","OneDrive.exe") by host Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
| where NOT match(file_path,"(?i)\\\\(Google\\\\Chrome|Microsoft\\\\Edge|Mozilla\\\\Firefox|Opera Software)\\\\")
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| table firstTime lastTime host user process_name file_name file_path count
ucs.kql:
// Ajax Security Team 'Stealer' — non-browser process copying browser credential stores
let BrowserBins   = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","setup.exe","msiexec.exe","OneDrive.exe","backgroundtaskhost.exe"]);
let BrowserPaths  = dynamic([@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\", @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\", @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\", @"\Opera Software\", @"\BraveSoftware\"]);
let CredFiles     = dynamic(["login data","web data","cookies","logins.json","key3.db","key4.db","signons.sqlite","cert8.db","cert9.db","places.sqlite"]);
let StealerFile = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
    | where tolower(FileName) in (CredFiles)
         or FileName matches regex @"(?i)(login.?data|signons|cookies)\.(sqlite|db|copy|bak|tmp)$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (BrowserBins)
    | where not(FolderPath has_any (BrowserPaths))   // copy lives OUTSIDE the legit profile dir
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName,
              ActorUser    = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              ActorProc    = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ActorCmd     = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ActorFolder  = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              DroppedFile  = strcat(FolderPath,"\\",FileName),
              ActorSHA256  = InitiatingProcessSHA256;
// Optional enrichment: same actor process also wrote to a typical keylog buffer (T1056.001 – Stealer keylogger artefacts)
let KeyLogDrop = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName matches regex @"(?i)(keylog|klog|kl_|stroke|.+_keys?)\.(txt|log|dat|bin)$"
         or FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Logs\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\")
    | project KeyLogTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, KeyActor = InitiatingProcessFileName, KeyFile = strcat(FolderPath,"\\",FileName);
StealerFile
| join kind=leftouter KeyLogDrop on $left.DeviceName == $right.DeviceName and $left.ActorProc == $right.KeyActor
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Ajax Security Team 'Stealer' — non-browser process copying browser credential stores (Sentinel ASIM port)
let BrowserBins  = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","setup.exe","msiexec.exe","OneDrive.exe"]);
let BrowserPaths = dynamic([@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\", @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\", @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\", @"\Opera Software\", @"\BraveSoftware\"]);
let CredFiles    = dynamic(["login data","web data","cookies","logins.json","key3.db","key4.db","signons.sqlite","cert8.db","cert9.db","places.sqlite"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
| where tolower(TargetFileName) in (CredFiles)
     or TargetFileName matches regex @"(?i)(login.?data|signons|cookies)\.(sqlite|db|copy|bak|tmp)$"
| extend ActorLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ActorLeaf !in (BrowserBins)
| where not(TargetFilePath has_any (BrowserPaths))
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, TargetFilePath, TargetFileName, TargetFileSHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Browser Credential Store Copied by Non-Browser Process (Ajax 'Stealer')
id: 91d4cf12-7c2e-49b5-bdd7-ajax02stealer
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects non-browser processes creating files named after Chrome/Edge/Firefox credential SQLite databases
  (Login Data, Web Data, logins.json, key3.db, key4.db, signons.sqlite) outside of the legitimate browser
  profile directory. This is the file-system signature of Ajax Security Team's 'Stealer' family
  (Operation Saffron Rose) staging browser credentials for exfiltration.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0130
  - https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2014/05/operation-saffron-rose.html
author: Detection Engineering (Ajax/Rocket-Kitten profile)
date: 2026/05/05
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_credfile:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\Login Data'
      - '\Web Data'
      - '\Cookies'
      - '\logins.json'
      - '\key3.db'
      - '\key4.db'
      - '\signons.sqlite'
      - '\cert8.db'
      - '\cert9.db'
      - '\places.sqlite'
  filter_browser_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\opera.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\iexplore.exe'
      - '\OneDrive.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\setup.exe'
  filter_browser_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\Google\Chrome\User Data\'
      - '\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\'
      - '\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\'
      - '\Opera Software\'
      - '\BraveSoftware\'
  condition: selection_credfile and not filter_browser_proc and not filter_browser_path
fields:
  - User
  - Image
  - TargetFilename
falsepositives:
  - Backup software performing user-profile snapshots (allowlist by signed publisher)
  - Browser-data migration tools run by helpdesk
level: high
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1555.003
  - attack.collection
  - attack.t1056.001
  - attack.g0130
ucs.rationale: FireEye's reporting on Operation Saffron Rose documents that Ajax Security Team's 'Stealer' implant performs browser credential theft by file-copying the underlying SQLite databases (Login Data, logins.json, key3.db) — not via DPAPI calls — and pairs it with a keylogger drop (T1056.001) on disk. Detecting the file-copy of these specific filenames by a non-browser process outside the browser profil
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
